words.def: no longer needed for a particular process and therefore thrown away
words.example: the disposal of waste material

words.def: a liquid without colour, smell or taste that falls as rain, is in lakes, rivers and seas, and is used for drinking, washing, etc.
words.example: a glass of water

words.def: a raised line of water that moves across the surface of the sea, ocean, etc.
words.example: Huge waves were breaking on the shore.

words.def: an object such as a knife, gun, bomb, etc. that is used for fighting or attacking somebody
words.example: Modern nuclear weapons are much more destructive than either biological or chemical weapons.

words.def: to have a particular weight
words.example: How much do you weigh (= how heavy are you)?

words.def: accepted or wanted somewhere
words.example: I'll go, then. I know when I'm not welcome.

words.def: at any time that; on any occasion that
words.example: You can ask for help whenever you need it.

words.def: used to express a doubt or choice between two possibilities
words.example: He seemed undecided whether to go or stay.

words.def: during the time that something is happening; at the same time as something else is happening
words.example: We must have been burgled while we were asleep.

words.def: full; complete
words.example: Let's forget the whole thing.

words.def: the ability to control your thoughts and actions in order to achieve what you want to do; a strong and determined desire to do something that you want to do
words.example: to have a strong will

words.def: one of the parts of the body of a bird, insect or bat that it uses for flying
words.example: The swan flapped its wings noisily.

words.def: before a particular period of time has passed; during a particular period of time
words.example: You should receive a reply within seven days.

words.def: a feeling of surprise and pleasure that you have when you see or experience something beautiful, unusual or unexpected
words.example: He retained a childlike sense of wonder.

words.def: the soft hair that covers the body of sheep and some other animals
words.example: Sheep were kept for their wool and meat.

words.def: having a job for which you are paid
words.example: the working population

words.def: the state of worrying about something
words.example: The threat of losing their jobs is a constant source of worry to them.

words.def: expressed in writing rather than in speech
words.example: Having a written record of what I've done is very valuable.

words.def: a piece of land next to or around your house where you can grow flowers, fruit, vegetables, etc., usually with a lawn (= an area of grass)
words.example: They have a gorgeous old oak tree in their front yard.

words.def: having lived or existed for only a short time; not fully developed
words.example: Young babies need to be wrapped up warmly.

words.def: the time of life when a person is young, especially the time before a child becomes an adult
words.example: in somebody's youth, He had been a talented musician in his youth.
